raw_user_request: Build a household budget tracker app.
concrete_goal: Build a browser-based household budget tracker for roommates who need to record shared expenses, see settlement status, and resolve overdue reimbursements from the first screen.
success_criteria: Roommates can add, edit, settle, and filter shared expense records without leaving the first screen.
The interface exposes payer, split members, due date, category, and settlement status with desktop and mobile evidence.
Empty, loading, error, validation, disabled, and saved-success states are represented or deliberately simulated.
scope_in: Single-page household budget tracker UI with local client-side state.
Seeded fictional roommate expense data and responsive controls.
scope_out: Real banking integrations, authentication, payment processing, and production deployment.
assumptions: The prototype uses fictional roommate and expense data.
Local browser state is acceptable for the first usable version.
implementation_constraints: Do not add external services, production deployment, or paid APIs.
Keep the primary expense workflow available on the first screen.

target_users: Roommates who split rent, utilities, groceries, and one-off household purchases and need a shared view of who owes whom.
product_brief.core_problem: Shared household expenses become hard to reconcile when payer, category, due date, and settlement status are scattered across chats or spreadsheets.
primary_workflows: Create or update a fictional shared expense with payer, participants, category, amount, due date, notes, and settlement status.
Filter expenses by overdue, unsettled, category, and payer so roommates can focus on reimbursement work.
Review empty, invalid, saved, and error states while preserving readable expense rows on desktop and mobile.
content_data_model_assumptions: Primary entity: expense record with fields id, payer, participants, category, amount, due date, note, settlement status, created time, and updated time.

Settlement records include member, owed amount, paid amount, and status values unpaid, partial, settled, or overdue; UI states include first-run empty list, loading seed data, save success, validation errors for missing payer or amount, disabled save while invalid, and recoverable local state error.

ux_workflow_completeness: Primary add/edit/filter expense workflow is usable from the first screen.
visual_polish: Settlement status, overdue state, and category hierarchy are visually distinct without generic placeholder cards.
responsive_desktop_mobile: Desktop table-style layout and mobile stacked layout are both inspected with concrete viewport evidence.
accessibility_basics: Controls have labels, visible focus, keyboard-reachable workflow, and sufficient contrast.
error_loading_empty_states: Empty, loading, error, validation, disabled, and saved-success states are represented.
text_overlap_layout_stability: Long roommate names, category labels, notes, and action controls wrap without overlap or clipping.
domain_fit: Labels, sample data, and interactions fit fictional shared household budgeting work.
evidence_requirements: Desktop visual inspection artifact or screenshot, mobile visual inspection artifact or screenshot, primary workflow behavior check, accessibility basics check, and state coverage check.

scenario_flows.name: Create shared expense
preconditions: The budget tracker is loaded with fictional seed data.
steps: Open the add expense form from the first screen.
Enter payer, participants, category, amount, due date, and note.
Save the record and confirm it appears with unsettled status.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The expense is visible with the selected split and can be filtered immediately.
failure_or_edge_cases: Missing payer or invalid amount shows validation and disables save.
A simulated save error preserves draft values and shows recovery guidance.

scenario_flows.name: Filter and settle overdue expenses
preconditions: At least one expense is overdue and one is settled.
steps: Mark one participant as settled and clear the filter.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Totals, visibility, and settlement labels update without hiding overdue items.
failure_or_edge_cases: Empty filter results show an empty state with a reset control.
Long notes wrap on mobile without covering controls.

verification_matrix.build: Run the repository build command when present or document why no build command exists.
verification_matrix.lint: Run lint or equivalent static checks when present.
verification_matrix.test: Run automated tests when present, or add a focused behavior test if the project has a test harness.
verification_matrix.run: Start the local app or open the static artifact and report the URL or file path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Exercise create, filter, validation, empty, error, desktop, mobile, and keyboard/focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing the expense tracker, filters, form, and detail area with no overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing stacked expense rows, reachable primary actions, and wrapped notes.
Behavior evidence for creating an expense, filtering overdue records, settling a participant, and observing updated totals.
Accessibility evidence covering labels, visible focus, keyboard operation, and contrast-sensitive settlement indicators.
State coverage evidence for empty, loading, error, saved-success, disabled, and validation states.

iteration_policy.threshold_rule: overall_completion_score must be at least quality_score_threshold.
iteration_policy.rework_rule: Any failed app-quality evidence creates concrete rework before acceptance.
stop_conditions: Review is accepted with all app evidence present.
A payment, external cost, or production deployment blocker is discovered.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Implement the first-screen household budget tracker workflow.
